sections.paragraph:
ἐλεήμων, ον gen. -ονος (s. ἔλεος; Hom.+) pert. to being concerned about people in their need, merciful, sympathetic, compassionate of God (so predom. LXX; EpArist 208; TestJud 19:3; Philo, Somn. 1, 93; PLond VI, 1917, 25 [c. 330–40]; Just. D. 107, 2.—Sb 8266, 19 [261/260 b.c.] of the god Amenophis; Apollod. [II b.c.]: 244 Fgm. 121 Jac. of Leto; IAndrosIsis 128; also of Isis: SEG VIII, 550, 34 [I b.c.]) w. οἰκτίρμων (as Ex 34:6 al. LXX; PLond VI, 1917, 20 and 22) 1 Cl 60:1. Of Christ (PGM 13, 290) Hb 2:17. Of people (Epict. 2, 21, 3; 5; 6; Ptolem., Apotel. 3, 14, 33; Herm. Wr. 1, 22; Ps 111:4; Pr 11:17; 20:6; TestSol 22:1 B; TestAbr A 4 p. 81, 3 [Stone p. 10]; TestSim 4:4; JosAs 8:9; Jos., Ant. 10, 41) Mt 5:7; 2 Cl 4:3; D 3:8. εἰς πάντας toward everyone Pol 6:1.—DELG s.v. ἔλεος. M-M. TW.

gen. gen. = genitive

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestSim TestSim = Testament of Simeon, s. Test12Patr—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 316
